title: Enterprise AI Market Tilts - TCR 04/12/26
description:
Anthropic surged to within striking distance of overtaking OpenAI in enterprise AI spending as cybersecurity professionals were given a six-month clock before AI-driven offensive vulnerability discovery proliferates beyond any single lab's control.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-12-2026/

Ramp's corporate spending data shows Anthropic now accounts for 30.6% of paying business customers while OpenAI holds 35.2%, with a projected crossover within two months. At the HumanX conference, executives described "Claude Mania" among enterprise buyers, and Cisco's president revealed that 85% of their 18,000-person engineering workforce now uses AI, framing the shift in terms of team composition rather than productivity. The cybersecurity arc deepened as Anthropic's offensive cyber research lead told NBC News that comparable vulnerability-chaining capabilities will be broadly distributed within six to twelve months, prompting the Cloud Security Alliance to convene an emergency huddle and CISOs to compress patch cycles from weeks to hours. India's Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam achieved criticality, advancing the country's full-stack sovereignty strategy across compute, chips, solar, and nuclear fuel. Volvo began serial production of the world's first electric articulated haul trucks - 39-tonne machines designed for full-day battery operation with deliveries to UK and Norwegian customers within weeks. UC Riverside chemists stabilized a carbene in liquid water for the first time, confirming a 67-year-old hypothesis about vitamin B1 and opening pharmaceutical synthesis pathways without toxic solvents. German researchers identified the mechanism by which the brain's immune cells destroy smell-related nerve fibers years before Alzheimer's symptoms appear, offering a potential diagnostic pathway a decade before cognitive decline.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-12-2026/
